you must add a specific binding redirect to your app configuration file to resolve the version mismatch issue between your net seven project and the presentation framework assembly
cousin relationships in code often mirror how different platforms handle shared libraries and you can use a similar approach to map the old version request to the newer one available in your system
the solution involves creating an app config or app settings file that tells the runtime to load version four point zero of the assembly even though your project targets a newer dot net version
you need to ensure your windows build includes the necessary runtime dependencies by adding a reference to the presentation framework package or by adjusting your project properties to include the correct target framework
this approach allows your cross platform application to remain compatible with both windows wpf and mac cocoa without requiring a full migration to the legacy dot net framework for the main application
